title: Use `and`/`or` in guards instead of `&&`/`||`
prompt:
The following has invalid Elixir (`&&` and `||` are not allowed in guards — they'll raise at compile or runtime in guard position):

```
def active?(user) when user.active && user.role != "banned", do: true
def active?(_), do: false

def contactable?(user) when user.email != nil || user.phone != nil, do: true
def contactable?(_), do: false
```

Fix by: (1) destructuring user in the head, (2) using `and`/`or` in guards, (3) asserting types properly (`is_binary`, not `!= nil`). Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.UserChecks do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Multiple issues to fix: illegal guard syntax, non-assertive nil checks, non-destructured access. Sonnet typically fixes one and leaves another.
research_citation: Elixir anti-patterns: 'Non-assertive Truthiness — Replace &&, ||, ! with and, or, not when operands are expected to be booleans.'
